locators: printed pp. 79-115

bibliographic.citation: Frederick Morgan Padelford, ‘English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26,’ Anglia: Zeitschrift für englische Philologie 36 (N.F. 24) (1912): 79-115.
bibliographic.title: English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26

dateStatements.label: article completion
dateStatements.notes: The dateline at the article's close records completion, not publication.

entities.label: Padelford 1912 edition
notes: The complete article occupies printed pages 79-115; CME00104 represents only pages 86-115.

Padelford's pages 80-85 discuss hands and dating. His claim that the orthography is not earlier than about 1570-1575 is retained as a historical editorial opinion and is not adopted as the cataloged work date.

rights.basis: The article was published in 1912, and U-M independently marks the derived CME item No Copyright-United States.
rights.component: Padelford's 1912 article, transcriptions, selection, and editorial apparatus
rights.jurisdiction: United States

dateStatements.label: music component
dateStatements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century; DIAMM refines the principal copying to 1425-1440 and about 1450

entities.description: A composite manuscript of five originally separate English manuscripts. The relevant music component is folios 3-33; Padelford's thirty-one selected English songs begin at current f. 5r and end with a later addition at f. 33v.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Arch. Selden B. 26
notes: The first selected English song begins at f. 5r; f. 4v contains Latin material.
Song 30 is on f. 33r and song 31, ‘Yf thow fle idelnes,’ is on f. 33v. The next recto begins unrelated Gregory material.
A separate faint four-line poem beginning ‘Hail blessed Lady’ appears in the lower margin of f. 23r. DIMEV 1699 catalogs it; Padelford and CME00104 omit it.

physicalDescription.extent: Composite codex; relevant music component folios 3-33
notes: DIAMM inventories 53 musical compositions in the component, including Latin and English pieces.
DIMEV records 32 English verse witnesses in the manuscript, one more than Padelford's numbered selection.
physicalDescription.support: Parchment for the Selden Carol Book component

rights.basis: The underlying texts predate modern copyright by centuries; this statement excludes modern images, descriptions, editions, and provider terms.
rights.component: Underlying medieval English, Latin, and French textual content
rights.jurisdiction: United States

next_steps: Search Anglia errata and later Padelford or Ryman scholarship for explicit corrections.
Retain both literal citations and the exact-text correction until independent documentary confirmation is found.
openQuestions.question: Can an errata notice or surviving Padelford working note independently confirm that ‘Archiv 82.206’ should read volume 89 and ‘Ee.1.2’ should read Ee.1.12?

next_steps: Compare the f. 33v hand and textual tradition with dated witnesses and current Selden Carol Book scholarship.
Keep Padelford's 1570-1575 view as a historical claim rather than a current date assertion.
openQuestions.question: Can the copying date and relationship of the later f. 33v song 31 be narrowed beyond DIMEV and DIAMM's approximate c. 1500 assessment?

next_steps: Request target or complete imaging from the British Library, Bodleian, and Cambridge University Library using the exact shelfmarks and locators.
Record item-specific reuse terms before redistributing any supplied images.
openQuestions.question: Can complete or target public facsimiles be obtained for Add MS 5665, Sloane MS 2593, the cited leaves of MS. Eng. poet. e. 1, and Cambridge Ee.1.12?

next_steps: Research and collate each DIMEV work record and its collateral witnesses separately.
Do not infer a single composition date or place from the Selden witness's copying context.
openQuestions.question: What are the individual composition dates, places, authorships, and textual relationships of Padelford's thirty-one heterogeneous songs?

next_steps: Keep all source-cache bundles Git-ignored and publish source URLs, checksums, and locators by default.
Treat Bodleian and Trinity CC BY-NC images as noncommercial and Balliol images as permission-required.
Obtain written permission before using any of those images in commercial outputs.
openQuestions.question: Which manuscript images may be redistributed in project outputs that can support commercial print-on-demand use?

reviewNotes: The preferred subject is Padelford's selected English songs plus the apparatus retained on printed pages 86-115, not the complete Anglia article, the complete Selden Carol Book music component, or the composite codex.
The source-song boundary is current folios 5r-33v / IIIF items 11-68. The preliminary 5r-33r boundary would omit numbered song 31.
The physical leaf range includes intervening Latin musical compositions that Padelford did not select; the thirty-one songs are distributed through the range rather than exhausting its contents.
Rights and access statements are entity-specific. In particular, medieval text, historical edition, digital encoding, and modern manuscript-image rights must not be conflated.
Negative public-copy findings are dated 2026-07-13 and do not prove that no private, licensed, or unindexed copy exists.

sourceLinks.accessMethod: Consult Cambridge University Library using shelfmark Ee.1.12; current public catalog and facsimile route not confirmed

lineage.summary: The checked-in CME00104 XML is a repository copy of the University of Michigan CME encoding of printed pages 86-115 of Frederick Morgan Padelford's 1912 Anglia article, not an encoding of the article's introductory pages 79-85. Those encoded pages comprise Padelford's thirty-one numbered English-song transcriptions from Bodleian MS Arch. Selden B. 26, distributed across current folios 5r-33v, together with interleaved variants and comparisons. Padelford transcribed the Selden texts directly, used four collateral manuscript witnesses directly, followed Fuller Maitland's 1891 edition for six Trinity Carol Roll versions, and drew three comparisons from Julius Zupitza's 1892 edition of Cambridge Ee.1.12. A complete Selden IIIF bundle proves that song 31 is the later poem on f. 33v, while DIMEV shows that Padelford omitted a separate four-line marginal poem on f. 23r. Literal Padelford references to Archiv volume 82 page 206 and Cambridge Ee.1.2 are retained as citation evidence but do not match the located source: the corresponding texts occur in Zupitza's volume 89 edition from Ee.1.12.

catalogSummary.title: English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26

catalogingSubject.label: English Songs in Manuscript Selden B. 26
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is Frederick Morgan Padelford's thirty-one numbered English-song transcriptions from Bodleian MS Arch. Selden B. 26 together with the interleaved variant and comparison apparatus represented by CME00104 on printed pages 86-115. It is not the complete 1912 article, whose title and introduction occupy pages 79-85; not all English verse in the Selden manuscript; not all 53 musical compositions in the relevant component; not the complete composite codex; and not the U-M encoding or repository XML as digital artifacts.

assertion.notes: The article closes with University of Washington, Dec. 3, 1910; this is not the publication date.

extent.description: The principal numbered extent is Padelford's 31 song sections. At XML-structure level those sections contain 31 direct source-song units with 186 LG and 717 L elements, while the interleaved apparatus contains 26 P elements, 68 further LG with 266 L, and four comparison L elements embedded in P, yielding 254 LG and 987 L overall. The prose-unit and verse-unit counts use different structural granularities and therefore are not summed to redefine the thirty-one numbered songs. These are encoded element counts, not canonical poem-line or stanza counts.

formStatements.description: Thirty-one verse source songs with modern prose apparatus and substantial quoted verse variants.
The thirty-one numbered Selden texts are songs in verse.
Modern prose notes plus complete or partial quoted verse versions and comparison lines.

scope.description: Many selected Selden songs and collateral versions use the medieval carol form; the label does not apply uniformly to every item
Nativity, Marian, penitential, salvation, and other sacred songs
Devotional songs directed to Christ, Mary, and Christian observance
Moral and conduct songs, including the f. 33v idleness quatrain
Love, social, work, drinking, historical, and conduct songs outside the principal sacred group
The Agincourt song and its Trinity-roll collateral version
Latin refrains, lines, headings, and comparison material within the selected songs and apparatus
French embedded in the multilingual song tradition, including the Latin-English-French carol identified in the Selden inventory

notes: The cataloging subject follows the intellectual selection actually encoded, while title and source-policy evidence may come from the article's omitted introductory pages.
Current recto/verso locators normalize Padelford's historical a/b foliation only where the manuscript object or matching source evidence supports the conversion.
The preferred language code is enm for the source songs. Latin and Middle French are embedded; modern English is the editorial language of the represented apparatus.
Witness date and copying provenance are not composition date and place. Song-level claims remain scoped and uncertain.
Rights and availability for manuscript images are recorded only in the lineage manifest and must not be inherited from the public-domain medieval texts or 1912 edition.

summaries.value: An edited selection of thirty-one anonymous Middle English songs transcribed by Frederick Morgan Padelford from the Selden Carol Book component of Bodleian MS Arch. Selden B. 26. Sacred carols and devotional lyrics predominate, but the selection also includes moral, social, work, drinking, love, historical, and conduct songs. Padelford interleaves substantive collateral versions and shorter readings from English manuscript witnesses and earlier printed editions.

CME00104 begins on Padelford's printed page 86 with song 1 and ends on page 115 with song 31, his dateline, and signature. It omits the article title, introductory hand and dating discussion, source-policy statement, and thirty-one-item first-line index on pages 79-85. At the manuscript layer the selected songs run from current f. 5r through f. 33v, but intervening Latin compositions and an independent marginal poem on f. 23r are outside the selection.

The apparatus contains twelve subordinate variant divisions attached to songs 3, 7, 8, 11, 12, 13, and 24, plus shorter comparison notes and four verse lines embedded inside prose apparatus for songs 10 and 11. Padelford uses Add MS 5665, MS Eng. poet. e. 1, Balliol MS 354, Sloane MS 2593, Fuller Maitland's Trinity Carol Roll edition, Zupitza's Jacob Ryman edition, Early Bodleian Music, and Early English Lyrics in distinct roles documented in the lineage manifest.

DIMEV catalogs thirty-two English verse witnesses in Arch. Selden B. 26, while DIAMM inventories fifty-three musical compositions in the relevant music component. Neither count contradicts the thirty-one-item cataloged subject: Padelford omitted the separate f. 23r marginal quatrain, selected English songs rather than every Latin and English composition, and included the later nonmusical f. 33v song as number 31.

notes: This is Padelford's article title and the preferred discovery title for the represented selection; it is not a collective title supplied by the Selden manuscript.
